title: Canonical Dynasty
description: A canonical political dynasty / ruling house in the Islamic Atlas knowledge graph. Phase 0 (v0.1.0) covers five concrete subtypes: Caliphate (universal-claim caliphal dynasties: Rāshidūn, Umayyad, Abbasid, Fāṭimid Ismā'īlī, Andalusian Umayyad after 929, Almohad), Sultanate (Saljūq, Ayyūbid, Mamlūk, Ottoman, etc.), Emirate (sub-caliphal and sub-sultanic ruling families: Aghlabid, Sāmānid, Hamdānid), Imamate (Zaydī, Ibāḍī, Twelver-Imāmī claim-bearing dynasties; distinct from caliphates by doctrinal claim), and Beylik (Anatolian post-Saljūq emirates and analogous principalities). Every dynasty carries multilingual labels, a temporal range (start/end years required: a dynasty without dates is not a dynasty for our purposes), an optional Bosworth NID reference (the primary tertiary-reference anchor), an optional list of capitals, predecessor/successor pointers for political continuity chains, an optional rulers list (Phase 0: minimal name + reign period; full prosopography in P0.2), an authority cross-reference block, and provenance.

@id.description: Persistent identifier in the iac:dynasty- namespace. Eight-digit zero-padded ordinal allocated by the PID minter at first canonicalization; never reassigned, never deleted. Resolves under https://w3id.org/islamicatlas/id/dynasty/<slug>.

@type.description: RDF type array. MUST contain 'iac:Dynasty' (the abstract supertype, subClassOf crm:E40_Legal_Body). MAY additionally contain at most one Phase 0 subtype: 'iac:Caliphate', 'iac:Sultanate', 'iac:Emirate', 'iac:Imamate', or 'iac:Beylik'. The five subtypes are mutually exclusive in Phase 0 (no dynasty has two simultaneously); transitions across categories (e.g., Almoravid emirate that became a quasi-caliphate) are modelled as successor relationships between two separate dynasty records, not as multi-typing.

dynasty_subtype.description: Discriminator field that mirrors the @type subtype, lower-cased and singular for ergonomic filtering. Optional but recommended. MUST be coherent with @type when present: dynasty_subtype='sultanate' iff '@type' contains 'iac:Sultanate'.

labels.description: Multilingual label bundle. The prefLabel sub-property is required by the referenced schema and MUST contain at least 'en' for Phase 0 dynasties (Bosworth's New Islamic Dynasties is the lingua-franca anchor); 'ar' and 'tr' are strongly recommended.

temporal.description: Temporal range of the dynasty's political existence. REQUIRED for all dynasties (unlike places, where missing temporal coverage signals continuity, missing temporal coverage on a dynasty signals an incomplete record). Either a CE anchor (start_ce + end_ce) OR an AH anchor (start_ah + end_ah) is sufficient; the canonicalization pipeline computes the other automatically when one is given. For dynasties whose end year is contested by sources, set end_ce/end_ah to the conventionally cited year and use approximation='disputed' with a note.

bosworth_id.description: Bosworth 'New Islamic Dynasties: A Chronological and Genealogical Manual' NID record identifier (1-186 in the second edition). Primary tertiary-reference anchor for Phase 0 dynasty canonicalization. The Bosworth pilot ETL (Hafta 2 of Phase 0) seeds the dynasty namespace by walking NID-001 through NID-186 in sequence. When present, this same identifier appears in provenance.derived_from with source_type='secondary_scholarly'.

predecessor.description: PID references to dynasties that preceded this one in political continuity. Captures cases of direct succession (Rāshidūn → Umayyad), conquest succession (Sāsānian → Umayyad over Iran is OUT of scope; only intra-Islamicate predecessors are recorded here), partition succession (Ilkhānid → Jalāyirid + Muẓaffarid + …), and overthrow succession. Bidirectional consistency with successor enforced by the post-canonicalization integrity check.

successor.description: PID references to dynasties that succeeded this one. Inverse of predecessor.

place.description: PID reference to the iac:place- record of the capital.

from_ce.description: CE year the capital was established as the dynasty's seat. Optional; omit if the dynasty had a single capital across its whole span.

to_ce.description: CE year the capital was relinquished. Omit when the dynasty held the capital until its terminal year (signal: end of dynasty.temporal range).

items.description: Capital-tenure record: which place served as capital, optionally for which sub-period.

had_capital.description: Capitals of the dynasty, each with optional sub-period tenure. Multiple records express capital relocations (Abbasid: Kūfa → Baghdād → Sāmarrā' → Baghdād) and co-capitals. Bidirectional consistency with place.had_capital_of enforced post-canonicalization.

territory.description: Territorial extent expressed as a list of PID references to iac:place- records of subtype 'region' or 'iqlim' that the dynasty controlled at its territorial maximum or characteristic extent. NOT a precise polygon; the canonical-store does not carry historical-political polygons in Phase 0 (those live in Phase 0.3+ as time-stamped territorial-extent records). Useful for coarse 'which dynasties ruled in Khurāsān ca. 900 CE' queries.

name.description: Ruler's name in conventional English/ALA-LC romanisation. Phase 0 minimal: just a string. In P0.2, when the iac:person- namespace activates, this becomes a PID reference and the inline name becomes a denormalised display copy.

name_ar.description: Ruler's name in Arabic script. Optional in Phase 0.

regnal_title.description: Regnal title (laqab / kunya commonly used in regnal contexts), e.g., 'al-Manṣūr', 'al-Mu'taṣim bi-llāh', 'Yıldırım Bāyezīd'.

reign_start_ce.description: Start of reign in CE. Required de facto (a ruler without a reign date is half-recorded).

person_pid.description: Forward-declared: PID reference to an iac:person- record, once the person namespace is live (Phase 0.2). In Phase 0 this field is unused; consumers MUST treat absence as 'not yet linked'.

note.description: Free-text editorial note (e.g., 'co-regent with brother al-Hādī during 785'; 'Bosworth NID-003 lists 36 rulers; this record covers only the first 12, the remainder pending').

items.description: Minimal ruler record for Phase 0. Order in the array MUST be chronological (earliest reign_start first); the canonicalization pipeline rejects out-of-order arrays.

rulers.description: Chronological list of rulers. Phase 0 carries inline minimal records (name + reign period); Phase 0.2 will deprecate the inline form in favour of person_pid references with the inline fields kept as denormalised display copies.

authority_xref.description: Cross-references to external authority files. Phase 0 actively reconciles Wikidata ('e.g., Q11707' for Abbasid Caliphate); Pleiades is generally not applicable for dynasties (it is a place-authority); VIAF is applicable for the dynasty-as-corporate-body and is included as forward-declaration but not actively reconciled in Phase 0.

provenance.description: Mandatory provenance block. For Phase 0 Bosworth-pilot dynasties, derived_from MUST contain at least one entry with source_id='bosworth-nid:NNN' and source_type='secondary_scholarly'.

note.description: Free-text editorial note. Kept short; longer commentary belongs in labels.description.<lang> as a properly-tagged multilingual paragraph.

$comment: Phase 0 hard rules (enforced by pipeline, not by JSON Schema): (a) temporal MUST contain at least start_ce or start_ah AND the corresponding end; (b) rulers array, when present, MUST be chronologically sorted by reign_start_ce (or _ah); (c) had_capital.place MUST resolve to existing place PIDs of subtype 'settlement' at integrity-check time; (d) for each capital, the inverse pointer place.had_capital_of MUST contain this dynasty's PID; (e) predecessor/successor MUST resolve to existing dynasty PIDs and the inverse must be consistent.
